purpose: Physical setup geometry and photo references for the final fixture-model capture run.

camera_view.coordinate_convention: left/right names mean image-left/image-right in the saved camera frame while the camera faces the wall from behind/near the fixture; do not interpret from behind-fixture aperture side
notes: Camera view contains the two aperture projection envelopes on the wall.
Top molding/shelf is visible and must be excluded from analysis ROI.
Use the fixture-facing camera view, not Desk View.

measurement_notes: Four pencil ticks around the image-left projection are the image-left box outer boundary.
Four pencil ticks around the image-right projection are the image-right box outer boundary.
Measurements are user-provided. Everything is approximate except values explicitly marked exact_user_measured.

fixture_geometry.aperture_relationship: one fixture has two apertures that produce mirror images of each other
fixture_geometry.capture_scope: only one fixture is used for the comprehensive capture because both fixtures are identical

manual_notes.ch3_ch4: CH3 selects static bank/folder; CH4 selects a new look every 5 values, with 255 also treated as terminal look.
manual_notes.ch6_ch7_visibility: CH6=128 and CH7=128 are required to keep the pattern visible except during CH6/CH7 sweeps.
manual_notes.static_aliasing: Static scanned outlines can appear to have a chasing bright segment in video due to scan/rolling-shutter aliasing; manual-derived static/motion intent should be used as a prior.
manual_notes.runtime_usage: The capture analyzer reads this file only while deriving captures/fixture_model/analysis_geometry.json from a static reference still: it aligns detected aperture-box height to the measured box height to compute px/inch scale. Frame stats and dense analysis consume analysis_geometry.json after that. This file remains reference data for later coordinate analysis: wall pixels -> real-world wall coordinates -> fixture scan-space.
